name: Jason Liu
bio: Jason Liu is a staff-level machine learning engineer, angel investor, a16z scout, and the creator of Instructor, a Python library for structured outputs from LLMs with 12,500+ GitHub stars and 6M+ monthly downloads. The OpenAI team cited Instructor as direct inspiration for their native structured output feature. Liu studied Computational Mathematics & Statistics at the University of Waterloo (2012-2017). He worked as a Data Scientist at Meta (Facebook) in 2017 on content detection algorithms at 2B+ user scale, then spent five years as a Staff ML Engineer at Stitch Fix (2018-2023), where he led a team of 6-7 engineers building multimodal embedding systems and created the Flight framework processing 350M+ daily requests. In 2023 he founded 567 Studios, a solo AI consulting practice advising seed-to-Series B startups including Zapier, HubSpot, Limitless AI, Weights & Biases, Modal Labs, Timescale, and Pydantic. He ran cohort-based training programs on Maven with students from OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Microsoft, Amazon, and McKinsey. In February 2026 he sunset 567 Labs and open-sourced all course content. He is based in New York.

timeline.event: Enrolled at University of Waterloo, Computational Mathematics & Statistics
Data Scientist at Meta (Facebook), building content detection algorithms at 2B+ user scale
Joined Stitch Fix as Staff ML Engineer; led multimodal AI and search team (6-7 engineers)
Open-sourced Instructor (originally 'OpenAI Function Call and Pydantic Integration Module') on GitHub
Left Stitch Fix; founded 567 Studios as an independent AI consultant
Appeared on Latent Space podcast: 'High Agency Pydantic > VC Backed Frameworks'
Appeared on devtools.fm podcast: 'Instructor, Shipping LLMs to Production'
Appeared on Weaviate Podcast #88 discussing Instructor and AI consulting
OpenAI launched native structured outputs, citing Instructor as inspiration
Launched Maven course 'Systematically Improving RAG Applications' (6-week cohort-based)
Instructor surpassed 6M+ monthly PyPI downloads and 12,000+ GitHub stars
Sunset 567 Labs; open-sourced all course content (RAG Playbook + Consulting Archive)

key_contributions.title: Instructor
key_contributions.description: Python library for structured outputs from LLMs, patching provider SDKs to return Pydantic models. 12,500+ GitHub stars, 6M+ monthly downloads, cited by OpenAI as inspiration for their structured output feature.

key_contributions.title: Instructor JS/TS
key_contributions.description: TypeScript port of Instructor for structured extraction from LLMs in the JavaScript ecosystem.

key_contributions.title: Flight Framework (Stitch Fix)
key_contributions.description: Internal ML pipeline framework at Stitch Fix processing 350M+ daily requests with 80% internal adoption, serving as a semantic bridge integrating multiple systems.

key_contributions.title: Systematically Improving RAG (Maven Course)
key_contributions.description: 6-week hands-on course covering synthetic evaluation, embedding fine-tuning for 20-40% gains, query segmentation, and multimodal indices. Students from OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Microsoft, Amazon.

key_contributions.title: 567 Labs Open-Source Course Archive
key_contributions.description: Open-sourced written content from RAG Playbook and Consulting courses as freely available ebooks after sunsetting 567 Labs.

key_contributions.title: Context Engineering for RAG
key_contributions.description: Prolific writing on building better agentic RAG systems, evaluation frameworks, and practical AI engineering patterns.

quotes.text: It's just Python, right? Like, if you're going to use the LLM SDKs, you're obviously going to install instructor.
Really, I care a lot more than just the fact that it is JSON. Function calling really shines when you can specify the schema.
I just don't think it's a billion dollar company. I think if I make a million dollars as a consultant, I'm super happy.
Agency is just a matter of having courage and doing the thing that's scary. The higher agency person just falls down and tries.
Most of the time you're not going to need someone writing PyTorch code. You're gonna need someone to write instructor code.
